title: security_headers middleware parses header values with .unwrap() on every response
description: In security_headers (server.rs lines 65-76), five header values are constructed with .parse().unwrap(). These are string literals that are valid HeaderValues and will never fail, but .unwrap() in middleware that runs on every response is a panic surface that would crash the server if a value were ever changed to something invalid during maintenance. The correct approach is to use static HeaderValue constants (via http::HeaderValue::from_static) which panic at compile time rather than at runtime, eliminating the per-response parse and making the panic impossible at runtime.
